description: When no dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients is configured and the client MAC is not learned then the first L2VPN DHCP client packet is dropped.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.17.1001, what caveat is documented for DHCP Relay?
Feature Caveat: DHCP Relay - When no dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients is configured and the client MAC is not learned then the first L2VPN DHCP client packet is dropped.

description: On 8325, 8325P and 8325H Switch series, iBGP split-horizon rule does not apply across address families For example, routes exchanged between VSX peers over iBGP may be re-advertised into other families, which can introduce routing loops. To prevent this behavior, route-maps (for example, using BGP communities) should be used to classify the routes and explicitly control their propagation. In particular, it is recommended to enforce policies using route-maps that restrict the advertisement of these routes into the EVPN address family.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.17.1001, what caveat is documented for BGP?

Feature Caveat: BGP - On 8325, 8325P and 8325H Switch series, iBGP split-horizon rule does not apply across address families For example, routes exchanged between VSX peers over iBGP may be re-advertised into other families, which can introduce routing loops. To prevent this behavior, route-maps (for example, using BGP communities) should be used to classify the routes and explicitly control their propagation. In particular, it is recommended to enforce policies using route-maps that restrict the advertisement of these routes into the EVPN address family.

description: When configuring the same BGP neighbor under both IPv4 and IPv6 address families on a router, if a maximum prefix limit is set on one address family (like IPv4), exceeding that limit can potentially impact the BGP session for the other address family (IPv6) as well.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.17.1001, what limitation is mentioned for BGP?

Feature Caveat: BGP - When configuring the same BGP neighbor under both IPv4 and IPv6 address families on a router, if a maximum prefix limit is set on one address family (like IPv4), exceeding that limit can potentially impact the BGP session for the other address family (IPv6) as well.

description: If there is no user configuration for DNS servers and HPE Aruba Networking Central is configured, the switch defaults to using Google's DNS server at 8.8.8.8. HPE Aruba Networking Central is enabled by default across all switch platforms.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.17.1001, what caveat is documented for Activate?

Feature Caveat: Activate - If there is no user configuration for DNS servers and HPE Aruba Networking Central is configured, the switch defaults to using Google's DNS server at 8.8.8.8. HPE Aruba Networking Central is enabled by default across all switch platforms.

description: Duplicate Address Detection (DAD) status indicated a duplicate for IPv6 anycast gateway configuration. As a workaround, configure an active gateway IPv6 address and SVI IPv6 address, as this can prevent DAD issues. Issuing the commands interface shut followed by interface no-shut can help in resolving the issue.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.17.1001, what caveat is documented for ARP?

Feature Caveat: ARP - Duplicate Address Detection (DAD) status indicated a duplicate for IPv6 anycast gateway configuration. As a workaround, configure an active gateway IPv6 address and SVI IPv6 address, as this can prevent DAD issues. Issuing the commands interface shut followed by interface no-shut can help in resolving the issue.

description: After an issue with duplicate IPs on two VXLAN tunnel endpoints (VTEPs) is resolved, the kernel will have the ARP entry, but the output of the show arp and show evpn mac-ip commands do not show that IP. As a workaround, flap the physical interface.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.17.1001, what limitation is mentioned for ARP?

Feature Caveat: ARP - After an issue with duplicate IPs on two VXLAN tunnel endpoints (VTEPs) is resolved, the kernel will have the ARP entry, but the output of the show arp and show evpn mac-ip commands do not show that IP. As a workaround, flap the physical interface.

description: The next-hop-unchanged option needs to be explicitly configured to preserve nexthop while advertising routes to eBGP peers, in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. For example: Copy router bgp

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.17.1001, what additional caveat is documented for BGP?

Feature Caveat: BGP - The next-hop-unchanged option needs to be explicitly configured to preserve nexthop while advertising routes to eBGP peers, in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. For example: Copy router bgp.

description: Symptom: Switch is unable to connect to Central. Scenario: This issue may occur when attempting to onboard switches that are connected to Central. The process connecting to YANG is creating more sockets than expected, leading to overload of the yang-resolverd server and subsequent disconnections. Workaround: Restart yang-resolverd daemon to reestablish the operation of the daemon. Restart hpe-restd daemon to make a clean connection to YANG after restarting the yang-resolverd daemon.

description: Symptom: SNMPv3 user password changes do not take effect, after deleting and re-creating the user with a new password, the device still accepts the old password and rejects the new one. Scenario: This issue occurs after deleting an existing SNMPv3 user and re-add the same username with a different 'auth-pass'; authentication succeeds only with the original password. Workaround: Create a new SNMPv3 user with the desired password and do not reuse the original username.
symptom: SNMPv3 user password changes do not take effect, after deleting and re-creating the user with a new password, the device still accepts the old password and rejects the new one.

workaround: Create a new SNMPv3 user with the desired password and do not reuse the original username.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.17.1001, what is the workaround for SNMP Bug 408309?
SNMP (Bug ID 408309): The documented workaround is: Create a new SNMPv3 user with the desired password and do not reuse the original username.

description: Symptom: During bulk configuration pushes from Central, ovsdb-server crashes and triggers an automatic switch reboot. Scenario: This issue occurs when a bulk config push is applied on a switch running 17.0001. Workaround: Avoid large multi-edit bulk pushes from Central; apply configuration changes in smaller batches and monitor.

description: Symptom: After upgrading to 10.17.0001, LAG member interfaces remain stuck in “Initializing” state. Scenario: This issue occurs during upgrade or reboot on 10.17.0001 when a LAG contains one or more member ports on a line card that is down. Workaround: Before upgrade/reboot, remove from the LAG any member ports that belong to the down line card (or bring that line card online). If already impacted, remove the down-LC member ports from the LAG; the remaining members come up and the LAG forms.

description: Symptom: After converting a vsx pair to multihomed VTEP pair, the Ethernet Segment[ES] members are not populated in show Ethernet Segment output. Scenario: This issue occurs when the vsx configuration is removed and replaced with Link Aggregation Group[LAG] using an Ethernet Segment Identifier[ESI]. The ES members remain empty in the show Ethernet Segment output after the conversion process.

description: Symptom: Policy configuration validation for ports using subnet-based Group-Based Policy (GBP) is not functioning as expected. Scenario: This issue occurs when enabling policy configuration on the 8325 platform. Specifically, it occurs if AAA (Authentication, Authorization, and Accounting) configurations are not disabled on ports set for subnet-based GBP, leading to potential authentication conflicts.

description: Symptom: CPU-transmitted packets are not subjected to actions configured in policies applied to route-only ports on egress. This includes traffic originating directly from the switch, such as pings, as well as traffic forwarded to the CPU as part of the initial learning of a traffic flow. Scenario: This issue occurs when a policy is applied to a route-only port on egress and CPU-transmitted packets egress the port. Workaround: If the policy has any drop actions, use an egress ACL on the port to block the traffic instead.
symptom: CPU-transmitted packets are not subjected to actions configured in policies applied to route-only ports on egress. This includes traffic originating directly from the switch, such as pings, as well as traffic forwarded to the CPU as part of the initial learning of a traffic flow.

workaround: If the policy has any drop actions, use an egress ACL on the port to block the traffic instead.

Classifier (Bug ID 408254): Symptom: CPU-transmitted packets are not subjected to actions configured in policies applied to route-only ports on egress. This includes traffic originating directly from the switch, such as pings, as well as traffic forwarded to the CPU as part of the initial learning of a traffic flow. Scenario: This issue occurs when a policy is applied to a route-only port on egress and CPU-transmitted packets egress the port. Workaround: If the policy has any drop actions, use an egress ACL on the port to block the traffic instead.

description: Symptom: Duplicate traffic observed for a short duration when MAC addresses are in unknown state. Traffic gets flooded over Ethernet Segment and VXLAN tunnels until MAC re-learning process completes. Scenario: This issue occurs when events like interface flaps, reboot in EVPN multihoming environment causes MAC address deletion and re-learning cycles. During relearning process, MAC addresses enter an unknown state creating temporary forwarding gaps.
